title: Command Lifecycle, Configuration, Pipeline, and Atomic Output · Behavior Case: Pipeline Stages
summary: The script reads arguments during import, mixes parsing with writes, partially replaces the public directory on failure, and cannot explain which configuration won. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pipeline stages increment in import-safe deterministic build pipeline. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practi…
objectives: Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.
Define defaults, project configuration, environment, flags, validation, provenance, and redacted effective configuration.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-cli-pipeline-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-cli-pipeline-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_cli_pipeline_5(stages: int = 10, completed: int = 10, import_safe: bool = True, staged_output: bool = True, prior_artifact_kept: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if stages < 8 or completed < 0:
        return False, completed, "pipeline-shape"
    if completed != stages:
        return False, completed, "partial-pipeline"
    if not import_safe or not staged_output:
        return False, completed, "effect-boundary"
    if not prior_artifact_kept:
        return False, completed, "destructive-output"
    assert completed == stages
    return True, completed, "atomic-pipeline"

Keep the artifact unchanged and rewrite the explanation so the current output appears intentional. That claim does not survive the repair the pipeline stages defect evidence boundary.

For repair the pipeline stages defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pipeline Stages model · Theory Pipeline Stages
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Pipeline Stages: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 6190 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pipeline Stages model · Theory Pipeline Stages.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pipeline Stages model · Theory Pipeline Stages, keep Pipeline Stages connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pipeline Stages model · Theory Pipeline Stages, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Pipeline Stages precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pipeline Stages model · Theory Pipeline Stages, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 6190 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to own an import-safe staged build pipeline that preserves the prior artifact on failure; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate discover, admit, decode, parse, validate, route, render, asset, verify, stage, and publish contracts.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Pipeline Stages model · Theory Pipeline Stages, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A long build function is simpler because data stays local.

checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: ssg-pipeline-stages-fb5vbp.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-pipeline-stages-fb5vbp(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_pipeline_stages_fb5vbp\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?stages < 8 or completed < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?completed != stages)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not import_safe or not staged_output)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not prior_artifact_kept)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
